title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S17
body:
**Scene 17: 21:00–22:30 · Walter Approaches the Door**

HERO FRAME: Walter from behind, walking slowly across the parlor toward the door · the door looming larger as we approach · the brass doorknob catching lamplight · Walter's shadow stretching across the floor

MOTION: kling — slow tracking dolly behind Walter as he steps. His shadow elongates and shortens with each step. The doorknob's brass catches the light then dims. His hand reaches toward the doorknob.

MOOD: The slowest approach in television.

AUDIO: Walter (whispered, to himself, repeated): *"There is no one. There is no one. There is no one."*

Then he says aloud, hoarsely: *"Who is there?"*

A second knock. KNOCK. KNOCK.

A voice from the other side, very gentle: *"A friend."*

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
